qa_pairs.question: What are the three control panels available on the Slurm portal page for viewing resource usage?
qa_pairs.answer: The three control panels available are 'Select system and dates', 'Parameters', and 'SLURM account'.

qa_pairs.question: How can users select a specific project account to display in the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can select a specific project account to display by using the 'Select user’s account' pull-down menu of the 'SLURM account' panel.

qa_pairs.question: What does the usage figure display if the 'Select user’s account' pull-down menu is left empty?
qa_pairs.answer: If the 'Select user’s account' pull-down menu is left empty, the figure will display all of your usage across accounts on the specified cluster during the selected time period.

qa_pairs.question: Which accounts are included in the 'Select user’s account' pull-down menu?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Select user’s account' pull-down menu is populated by a list of all the accounts that have job records on the selected cluster during the selected time interval. Other accounts that you have access to but do not have usage on the selected cluster during the selected time interval will also appear in the pull-down menu but will be grayed out and not selectable.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after a single project account is selected for display?
qa_pairs.answer: When a single project account is selected, the figure is updated, and the summary panel titled 'Allocation Information' is populated with details of the project account.

qa_pairs.question: How is the usage for a specified account represented in the histogram figure?
qa_pairs.answer: The height of each bar in the histogram figure corresponds to the metric for that day (e.g., CPU-equivalent days) across all users in the account on the system.

qa_pairs.question: How are individual users' contributions displayed in the usage histogram?
qa_pairs.answer: The top seven users are displayed in unique colours stacked on top of the summed metric for all other users in gray.

qa_pairs.question: What tools can be used to navigate the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can navigate the figure using Plotly tools (zoom, pan, etc.) whose icons appear at the top-right when hovering the mouse over the figure window. The legend on the right-hand side can also be used to manipulate the figure.

qa_pairs.question: How do you manipulate items using the legend on the right-hand side of the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: Single-clicking an item will toggle the item's presence in the figure, and double-clicking the item will toggle off or on all the other items in the figure.

qa_pairs.question: What additional variables can be displayed or hidden from the usage figure legend?
qa_pairs.answer: Beyond displaying the colour affiliation of each user, the legend provides access to displaying 'SLURM Raw Usage', 'SLURM Raw Shares', 'CCDB allocation', 'Queued jobs', and the daily 'Total'.

qa_pairs.question: Where do 'SLURM Raw Usage' and 'SLURM Raw Shares' metrics come from?
qa_pairs.answer: 'SLURM Raw Usage' and 'SLURM Raw Shares' are obtained from a poll of 'sshare' for each account on the clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What does 'CCDB allocation' represent in the usage figure legend?
qa_pairs.answer: 'CCDB allocation' is the account profile at CCDB representation of the 'SLURM Raw Shares'.

qa_pairs.question: How are 'Queued jobs' represented in the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: 'Queued jobs' is a metric that represents the quantity of resources belonging to jobs that are pending in the job queue and is represented with narrow gray bars for each day in the figure window.

qa_pairs.question: What function does the 'Total' option in the figure legend provide?
qa_pairs.answer: 'Total' adds text at the top of each bar, indicating the daily total of the metric across users for the day.

qa_pairs.question: How is the 'SLURM Raw Shares' displayed when a single account is selected?
qa_pairs.answer: When a single account has been selected for display, the 'SLURM Raw Shares' is shown as a horizontal red line.

qa_pairs.question: How can the display of the 'SLURM Raw Shares' be toggled?
qa_pairs.answer: It can be turned off or on with the 'Display allocation target by default' item in the 'Parameters' panel, or by clicking on 'SLURM Raw Shares' in the legend to the right of the figure.

qa_pairs.question: How can the 'Queued jobs' metric be toggled on or off?
qa_pairs.answer: You can toggle the display of the 'Queued jobs' metric by clicking on the words 'Queued jobs' in the legend to the right of the figure.

qa_pairs.question: What interactive options become available when hovering the mouse over the figure window?
qa_pairs.answer: Native Plotly interactive figure options are made available at the top right of the figure window when the mouse pointer hovers over the figure. These include icons for 'Download plot as a png', 'Zoom', 'Pan', 'Box Select', 'Lasso Select', 'Zoom in', 'Zoom out', 'Autoscale', and 'Reset axes'.

qa_pairs.question: What information appears in the hover text when the mouse pointer is over a bar item in the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: When hovering over bar items in the figure window, a hover text appears providing the 'User Name', 'Day', and 'Usage' quantity of the specific item under the pointer (note that this returns the usage quantity for the specific user not the sum across users for the day).
